ayahs.tafsirSummary: The angels said: Are you amazed at the decree of Allah? May the mercy of Allah and His blessings be upon you, people of the house. Indeed He is Praiseworthy and Honorable. Even today we send blessings upon them in our prayers.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The house of Ibrahim was blessed with mercy and with the best of tidings; we remember them in our supplications.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: She expressed amazement at giving birth in old age.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: When the fright left Ibrahim and the good tidings reached him he began to argue concerning the people of Lot.

ayahs.arabic: فَلَمَّا ذَهَبَ عَنْ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ ٱلرَّوْعُ وَجَآءَتْهُ ٱلْبُشْرَىٰ يُجَـٰدِلُنَا فِى قَوْمِ لُوطٍ ٧٤
ayahs.translation: And when the fright had left Abraham and the good tidings had reached him, he began to argue [i.e., plead] with Us concerning the people of Lot.
ayahs.summary: When the fright had left Ibrahim and the good tidings had reached him he began to argue with Us concerning the people of Lot.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Once he knew they were angels and had received the good news of his son, he was worried about the people of Lot—requesting more time or that they not be punished. The prophets were not selfish; they cared for humanity. When we enjoy blessings we must not forget those in need—materially and spiritually. Ibrahim was concerned for humanity.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Even in his own joy Ibrahim pleaded for others. We should care for people's spiritual and material needs.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The angels sent mercy and blessings upon the house.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Allah praises Ibrahim: indeed he was forbearing, grieving, and frequently returning.

ayahs.arabic: إِنَّ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ لَحَلِيمٌ أَوَّٰهٌۭ مُّنِيبٌۭ ٧٥
ayahs.translation: Indeed, Abraham was forbearing, grieving and [frequently] returning [to Allāh].
ayahs.summary: Indeed Ibrahim was forbearing, grieving, and frequently returning to Allah.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Indeed Ibrahim was forbearing, softhearted, and frequently returning to Allah. We need to ask: how much of our time, money, and effort are for our pleasures and how much for the welfare of humanity?
ayahs.lessonReflection: Ibrahim was gentle, concerned for people, and always turning back to Allah. We should balance our own life with concern for others.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Ibrahim argued with Us concerning the people of Lot.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: The angels said: O Ibrahim, give up this plea.

ayahs.arabic: يَـٰٓإِبْرَٰهِيمُ أَعْرِضْ عَنْ هَـٰذَآ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ قَدْ جَآءَ أَمْرُ رَبِّكَ ۖ وَإِنَّهُمْ ءَاتِيهِمْ عَذَابٌ غَيْرُ مَرْدُودٍۢ ٧٦
ayahs.translation: [The angels said], "O Abraham, give up this [plea]. Indeed, the command of your Lord has come, and indeed, there will reach them a punishment that cannot be repelled."
ayahs.summary: O Ibrahim, give up this plea. Indeed the command of your Lord has come and there will reach them a punishment that cannot be repelled.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: He is told there is no point—the people of Lot were given their chance. The command of your Lord has come; there will reach them a punishment that cannot be repelled. The people of Lot were committing a crime none before them had done—homosexuality. The Prophet said: The thing I most fear for my nation is the action of the people of Lot. The act is forbidden because it brings more harm than good; Allah has set limits for every pleasure.
ayahs.lessonReflection: When Allah's command has come, the punishment cannot be repelled. We must fear for our communities the spread of the worst sins.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Ibrahim was described as forbearing and frequently returning.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: When the messengers came to Lot he was anguished and said: This is a trying day.

ayahs.arabic: وَلَمَّا جَآءَتْ رُسُلُنَا لُوطًۭا سِىٓءَ بِهِمْ وَضَاقَ بِهِمْ ذَرْعًۭا وَقَالَ هَـٰذَا يَوْمٌ عَصِيبٌۭ ٧٧
ayahs.translation: And when Our messengers, [the angels], came to Lot, he was anguished for them and felt for them great discomfort and said, "This is a trying day."
ayahs.summary: When Our messengers came to Lot he was anguished for them and felt great discomfort and said: This is a trying day.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: They came in the form of men so he was afraid his people would harm them. When a man looks at another man as an object of sexual pleasure, where can anyone feel secure? Lot was instantly worried—his people would approach these guests; there was no modesty left in that society.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Lot's anguish shows the danger when a society loses modesty and preys on guests; it is a trying day.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Ibrahim was told to give up his plea.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: His people came hastening to him; he said: These are my daughters, fear Allah.

ayahs.arabic: وَجَآءَهُۥ قَوْمُهُۥ يُهْرَعُونَ إِلَيْهِ وَمِن قَبْلُ كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ ٱلسَّيِّـَٔاتِ ۚ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ بَنَاتِى هُنَّ أَطْهَرُ لَكُمْ ۖ فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَلَا تُخْزُونِ فِى ضَيْفِىٓ ۖ أَلَيْسَ مِنكُمْ رَجُلٌۭ رَّشِيدٌۭ ٧٨
ayahs.translation: And his people came hastening to him, and before [this] they had been doing evil deeds. He said, "O my people, these are my daughters; they are purer for you. So fear Allāh and do not disgrace me concerning my guests. Is there not among you a man of reason?"
ayahs.summary: His people came hastening; they had been doing evil. He said: O my people, these are my daughters—purer for you. Fear Allah and do not disgrace me concerning my guests. Is there not among you a man of reason?
ayahs.tafsirSummary: They came rushing—no modesty. They had already been doing evil but when they saw these men they wanted more. Haram never satisfies; halal brings contentment. He said: These are my daughters (or the women of the nation); they are purer for you. Fear Allah and do not disgrace me concerning my guests.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Lot offered the halal option and appealed to their fear of Allah and to reason; they refused.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Lot said this is a trying day.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: They said: You know we have no claim concerning your daughters and you know what we want.

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوا۟ لَقَدْ عَلِمْتَ مَا لَنَا فِى بَنَاتِكَ مِنْ حَقٍّۢ وَإِنَّكَ لَتَعْلَمُ مَا نُرِيدُ ٧٩
ayahs.translation: They said, "You have already known that we have not concerning your daughters [i.e., women] any claim [i.e., desire], and indeed, you know what we want."
ayahs.summary: They said: You have known we have no claim concerning your daughters and you know what we want.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: They said: You know we have no interest in women—we don't find them attractive. You know what pleases us, you know we like men. The exact same words are said today by those who commit this sin. They had been doing haram and were not satisfied; when they saw the angels they wanted more.
ayahs.lessonReflection: They openly declared their corrupt desire; haram never satisfies—it only demands more.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Lot offered his daughters and asked for a man of reason.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Lot said: If only I had power over you or a strong support.

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ لَوْ أَنَّ لِى بِكُمْ قُوَّةً أَوْ ءَاوِىٓ إِلَىٰ رُكْنٍۢ شَدِيدٍۢ ٨٠
ayahs.translation: He said, "If only I had against you some power or could take refuge in a strong support."
ayahs.summary: He said: If only I had against you some power or could take refuge in a strong support.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Meaning: If I could fix you by force I would—Lot was all alone; even his wife did not believe. He had no power to stop them.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The prophet wished he had strength to stop the wrong, but he could only warn and rely on Allah.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: They declared what they wanted.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: The angels said: O Lot, we are messengers of your Lord; they will never reach you.

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوا۟ يَـٰلُوطُ إِنَّا رُسُلُ رَبِّكَ لَن يَصِلُوٓا۟ إِلَيْكَ ۖ فَأَسْرِ بِأَهْلِكَ بِقِطْعٍۢ مِّنَ ٱلَّيْلِ وَلَا يَلْتَفِتْ مِنكُمْ أَحَدٌ إِلَّا ٱمْرَأَتَكَ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ مُصِيبُهَا مَآ أَصَابَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّ مَوْعِدَهُمُ ٱلصُّبْحُ ۚ أَلَيْسَ ٱلصُّبْحُ بِقَرِيبٍۢ ٨١
ayahs.translation: They [the angels] said, "O Lot, indeed we are messengers of your Lord; [therefore], they will never reach you. So set out with your family during a portion of the night and let not any among you look back - except your wife; indeed, she will be struck by that which strikes them. Indeed, their appointment is [for] the morning. Is not the morning near?"
ayahs.summary: They said: O Lot, we are messengers of your Lord; they will never reach you. Set out with your family in a portion of the night; let none look back except your wife—she will be struck. Their appointment is the morning.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: We are angels; you don't need to worry about us—they will never reach us. Set out with your family during a portion of the night and do not look back except your wife; she will look back and will be struck. Their appointment is the morning.
ayahs.lessonReflection: When the command comes, the believers must leave and not look back; only those who obey are saved.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Lot said he had no power.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: When the command came the highest part was made lowest and stones of layered clay were rained upon them.

ayahs.arabic: فَلَمَّا جَآءَ أَمْرُنَا جَعَلْنَا عَـٰلِيَهَا سَافِلَهَا وَأَمْطَرْنَا عَلَيْهَا حِجَارَةًۭ مِّن سِجِّيلٍۢ مَّنضُودٍۢ ٨٢
ayahs.translation: So when Our command came, We made the highest part [of the city] its lowest and rained upon them stones of layered hard clay, [which were]
ayahs.summary: When Our command came We made the highest part its lowest and rained upon them stones of layered hard clay.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: When Our command came We made the highest part its lowest and rained upon them stones of layered hard clay—marked from your Lord. Allah's punishment is not far from the wrongdoers who indulge in that sin.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The city was overturned and stones rained down; the punishment was from Allah and was not far from the wrongdoers.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Lot was told to leave by night and not look back.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Marked from your Lord; not far from the wrongdoers. Then Shu'ayb is sent to Madyan.

ayahs.arabic: مُّسَوَّمَةً عِندَ رَبِّكَ ۖ وَمَا هِىَ مِنَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ بِبَعِيدٍۢ ٨٣
ayahs.translation: Marked from your Lord. And it [i.e., Allāh's punishment] is not from the wrongdoers [very] far.
ayahs.summary: Marked from your Lord. And it is not from the wrongdoers far.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: The stones were marked from your Lord. And His punishment is not far from the wrongdoers—those who indulge in that wrong.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Allah's punishment is marked and not far from those who persist in wrong.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Stones of layered clay were rained upon them.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: To Madyan We sent their brother Shu'ayb.

ayahs.arabic: ۞ وَإِلَىٰ مَدْيَنَ أَخَاهُمْ شُعَيْبًۭا ۚ قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْبُدُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَـٰهٍ غَيْرُهُۥ ۖ وَلَا تَنقُصُوا۟ ٱلْمِكْيَالَ وَٱلْمِيزَانَ ۚ إِنِّىٓ أَرَىٰكُم بِخَيْرٍۢ وَإِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذَابَ يَوْمٍۢ مُّحِيطٍۢ ٨٤
ayahs.translation: And to Madyan [We sent] their brother Shuʿayb. He said, "O my people, worship Allāh; you have no deity other than Him. And do not decrease from the measure and the scale. Indeed, I see you in prosperity, but indeed, I fear for you the punishment of an all-encompassing Day.
ayahs.summary: To Madyan We sent their brother Shu'ayb. He said: Worship Allah; you have no deity other than Him. Do not decrease the measure and the scale. I see you in prosperity but I fear for you the punishment of an all-encompassing Day.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: The people of Shu'ayb were believers but when they traded they cheated. He said: I see you in prosperity—you already have much; why earn more through haram? When a person is greedy for haram he is not satisfied. We should ask Allah for contentment with what He has provided and that we only find halal pleasing.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Cheating in measure and scale is haram; prosperity does not justify it. Ask Allah for contentment with halal.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The punishment was not far from the wrongdoers.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Shu'ayb said: Give full measure and weight in justice; do not deprive people or spread corruption.

ayahs.arabic: وَيَـٰقَوْمِ أَوْفُوا۟ ٱلْمِكْيَالَ وَٱلْمِيزَانَ بِٱلْقِسْطِ ۖ وَلَا تَبْخَسُوا۟ ٱلنَّاسَ أَشْيَآءَهُمْ وَلَا تَعْثَوْا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ ٨٥
ayahs.translation: And O my people, give full measure and weight in justice and do not deprive the people of their due and do not commit abuse on the earth, spreading corruption.
ayahs.summary: O my people, give full measure and weight in justice; do not deprive people of their due or commit abuse on the earth, spreading corruption.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Give full measure and weight in justice; do not deprive people of their due; do not commit abuse on the earth spreading corruption. Cheating in weights and measures is haram and a major sin. Abdullah bin Umar said: They do not cheat in weights and measures but that they will be struck with famine and the oppression of rulers. To cheat another is not cleverness—it is pure foolishness.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Cheating in trade brings Allah's punishment—famine and oppression. Justice in measure and scale is obligatory.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Shu'ayb called them to worship Allah and not decrease measure and scale.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: What remains lawful from Allah is best; but I am not a guardian over you.

ayahs.arabic: بَقِيَّتُ ٱللَّهِ خَيْرٌۭ لَّكُمْ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ ۚ وَمَآ أَنَا۠ عَلَيْكُم بِحَفِيظٍۢ ٨٦
ayahs.translation: What remains [lawful] from Allāh is best for you, if you would be believers. But I am not a guardian over you."
ayahs.summary: What remains from Allah is best for you if you would be believers. But I am not a guardian over you.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: What is lawful from Allah, even if little, is good and will have barakah. But I am not a guardian over you—I cannot force you.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Halal provision, however little, is better than haram; the prophet can only advise, not force.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Shu'ayb told them to give full measure and not spread corruption.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: They said: Does your prayer command that we leave what our fathers worship?

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوا۟ يَـٰشُعَيْبُ أَصَلَوٰتُكَ تَأْمُرُكَ أَن نَّتْرُكَ مَا يَعْبُدُ ءَابَآؤُنَآ أَوْ أَن نَّفْعَلَ فِىٓ أَمْوَٰلِنَا مَا نَشَـٰٓؤُا۟ ۖ إِنَّكَ لَأَنتَ ٱلْحَلِيمُ ٱلرَّشِيدُ ٨٧
ayahs.translation: They said, "O Shuʿayb, does your prayer [i.e., religion] command you that we should leave what our fathers worship or not do with our wealth what we please? Indeed, you are the forbearing, the discerning!"
ayahs.summary: They said: O Shu'ayb, does your prayer command that we leave what our fathers worship or not do with our wealth what we please? You are the forbearing, the discerning.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: They taunted him for his prayer and religion—does it command that we leave what our fathers worship or not do with our wealth what we please? You are the forbearing, the discerning. They blindly followed their forefathers and accused him of extremism.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Rejecters mock the religion and the prophet's character while clinging to their fathers' way.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Shu'ayb said he was not a guardian.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Shu'ayb said: I am upon clear evidence; I only intend reform; my success is through Allah.

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ أَرَءَيْتُمْ إِن كُنتُ عَلَىٰ بَيِّنَةٍۢ مِّن رَّبِّى وَرَزَقَنِى مِنْهُ رِزْقًا حَسَنًۭا ۚ وَمَآ أُرِيدُ أَنْ أُخَالِفَكُمْ إِلَىٰ مَآ أَنْهَىٰكُمْ عَنْهُ ۚ إِنْ أُرِيدُ إِلَّا ٱلْإِصْلَـٰحَ مَا ٱسْتَطَعْتُ ۚ وَمَا تَوْفِيقِىٓ إِلَّا بِٱللَّهِ ۚ عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ وَإِلَيْهِ أُنِيبُ ٨٨
ayahs.translation: He said, "O my people, have you considered: if I am upon clear evidence from my Lord and He has provided me with a good provision from Him...? And I do not intend to differ from you in that which I have forbidden you; I only intend reform as much as I am able. And my success is not but through Allāh. Upon Him I have relied, and to Him I return.
ayahs.summary: He said: If I am upon clear evidence and He has provided me good provision—I do not intend to differ from you in what I have forbidden; I only intend reform. My success is only through Allah; upon Him I have relied and to Him I return.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: When a person finds that Allah is giving him halal that is sufficient, he should remember: my success is only through Allah; upon Him I have relied and to Him I return. I only intend reform as much as I am able.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Our provision and success are from Allah; we rely on Him and return to Him, and we only seek reform.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: They taunted him about his religion.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Do not let your dissension from me cause you to be struck as the people of Nuh, Hud, Salih, or Lot were.

ayahs.arabic: وَيَـٰقَوْمِ لَا يَجْرِمَنَّكُمْ شِقَاقِىٓ أَن يُصِيبَكُم مِّثْلُ مَآ أَصَابَ قَوْمَ نُوحٍ أَوْ قَوْمَ هُودٍ أَوْ قَوْمَ صَـٰلِحٍۢ ۚ وَمَا قَوْمُ لُوطٍۢ مِّنكُم بِبَعِيدٍۢ ٨٩
ayahs.translation: And O my people, let not [your] dissension from me cause you to be struck by that similar to what struck the people of Noah or the people of Hūd or the people of Ṣāliḥ. And the people of Lot are not from you far away.
ayahs.summary: O my people, let not your dissension from me cause you to be struck as the people of Nuh, Hud, or Salih were. The people of Lot are not far from you.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Do not let your opposition to me cause you to be struck by what struck the people of Nuh, Hud, or Salih. And the people of Lot are not far from you—their fate is a recent example.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Past nations were destroyed for rejecting their prophets; we are not far from the same fate if we persist.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Shu'ayb spoke of his reliance on Allah.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Ask forgiveness of your Lord and repent; my Lord is Merciful and Affectionate.

ayahs.arabic: وَٱسْتَغْفِرُوا۟ رَبَّكُمْ ثُمَّ تُوبُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِ ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّى رَحِيمٌۭ وَدُودٌۭ ٩٠
ayahs.translation: And ask forgiveness of your Lord and then repent to Him. Indeed, my Lord is Merciful and Affectionate."
ayahs.summary: And ask forgiveness of your Lord and then repent to Him. Indeed my Lord is Merciful and Affectionate.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: He reminds them of Allah's names—He is loving and affectionate; He made you and gave you much. Won't you reciprocate by being obedient? Whatever has happened in the past, if you seek forgiveness and beg His pardon, He will show you pardon.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Allah is Merciful and Affectionate; seek forgiveness and repent so He may pardon you.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Shu'ayb warned them not to be struck as previous nations.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: They said: We do not understand much of what you say; we consider you weak.

ayahs.arabic: قَالُوا۟ يَـٰشُعَيْبُ مَا نَفْقَهُ كَثِيرًۭا مِّمَّا تَقُولُ وَإِنَّا لَنَرَىٰكَ فِينَا ضَعِيفًۭا ۖ وَلَوْلَا رَهْطُكَ لَرَجَمْنَـٰكَ ۖ وَمَآ أَنتَ عَلَيْنَا بِعَزِيزٍۢ ٩١
ayahs.translation: They said, "O Shuʿayb, we do not understand much of what you say, and indeed, we consider you among us as weak. And if not for your family, we would have stoned you [to death]; and you are not to us one respected."
ayahs.summary: They said: O Shu'ayb, we do not understand much of what you say; we consider you weak. If not for your family we would have stoned you; you are not to us one respected.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: How ironic—to cheat in business requires cleverness, yet they said we do not understand. They considered him weak and said if not for your family we would have stoned you. When people cheat they suffer from famine and what they have becomes less. We must avoid a scarcity mentality and remember the many blessings we receive.
ayahs.lessonReflection: They mocked his clarity and his strength; the prophet's protection was from Allah and his family.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Shu'ayb invited them to seek forgiveness; his Lord is Merciful and Affectionate.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Shu'ayb said: Is my family more respected by you than Allah? You put Him behind your backs.

ayahs.arabic: قَالَ يَـٰقَوْمِ أَرَهْطِىٓ أَعَزُّ عَلَيْكُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَٱتَّخَذْتُمُوهُ وَرَآءَكُمْ ظِهْرِيًّا ۖ إِنَّ رَبِّى بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ مُحِيطٌۭ ٩٢
ayahs.translation: He said, "O my people, is my family more respected for power by you than Allāh? But you put Him behind your backs [in neglect]. Indeed, my Lord is encompassing of what you do.
ayahs.summary: He said: O my people, is my family more respected by you than Allah? You put Him behind your backs. My Lord is encompassing of what you do.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Is my family more respected for power by you than Allah? Today also what is of more concern to people—Allah's status or the status of people? They put Him behind their backs. Indeed my Lord is encompassing of what you do.
ayahs.lessonReflection: When we fear people more than Allah and put Him behind our backs, we are wrong. He is encompassing of all we do.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: They said they would have stoned him and he was not respected.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Work according to your position; I am working. You will know who receives disgrace and who is a liar.

ayahs.arabic: وَيَـٰقَوْمِ ٱعْمَلُوا۟ عَلَىٰ مَكَانَتِكُمْ إِنِّى عَـٰمِلٌۭ ۖ سَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ مَن يَأْتِيهِ عَذَابٌۭ يُخْزِيهِ وَمَنْ هُوَ كَـٰذِبٌۭ ۖ وَٱرْتَقِبُوٓا۟ إِنِّى مَعَكُمْ رَقِيبٌۭ ٩٣
ayahs.translation: And O my people, work according to your position; indeed, I am working. You are going to know to whom will come a punishment that will disgrace him and who is a liar. So watch; indeed, I am with you a watcher, [awaiting the outcome]."
ayahs.summary: O my people, work according to your position; I am working. You will know who will get a punishment that disgraces him and who is a liar. So watch; I am with you a watcher.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Work according to your position; I am working. You are going to know who will get a punishment that will disgrace him and who is a liar. So watch; I am with you a watcher awaiting the outcome.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The prophet does his work; the outcome will show who was disgraced and who was truthful.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Shu'ayb said they put Allah behind their backs.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: When Our command came We saved Shu'ayb and the believers; the shriek seized those who had wronged.

ayahs.arabic: وَلَمَّا جَآءَ أَمْرُنَا نَجَّيْنَا شُعَيْبًۭا وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مَعَهُۥ بِرَحْمَةٍۢ مِّنَّا وَأَخَذَتِ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ ٱلصَّيْحَةُ فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ فِى دِيَـٰرِهِمْ جَـٰثِمِينَ ٩٤
ayahs.translation: And when Our command came, We saved Shuʿayb and those who believed with him, by mercy from Us. And the shriek seized those who had wronged, and they became within their homes [corpses] fallen prone
ayahs.summary: When Our command came We saved Shu'ayb and those who believed with him by mercy from Us. The shriek seized those who had wronged and they became within their homes fallen prone.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: When Our command came We saved Shu'ayb and those who believed with him by mercy from Us. The shriek seized those who had wronged and they became within their homes fallen prone.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The believers are saved by mercy; the wrongdoers are seized by the shriek and fall in their homes.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Shu'ayb said he was a watcher with them.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: As if they had never prospered; away with Madyan as Thamud was taken away.

ayahs.arabic: كَأَن لَّمْ يَغْنَوْا۟ فِيهَآ ۗ أَلَا بُعْدًۭا لِّمَدْيَنَ كَمَا بَعِدَتْ ثَمُودُ ٩٥
ayahs.translation: As if they had never prospered therein. Then, away with Madyan as Thamūd was taken away.
ayahs.summary: As if they had never prospered therein. Away with Madyan as Thamud was taken away.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: As if they had never prospered therein. Then away with Madyan as Thamud was taken away. Part D (Hud 96–123) begins with Musa.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Nations that reject and cheat are removed as if they had never prospered.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The shriek seized them and they fell in their homes.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: We sent Musa with Our signs and a clear authority to Pharaoh and his establishment.

ayahs.arabic: وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا مُوسَىٰ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا وَسُلْطَـٰنٍۢ مُّبِينٍ ٩٦
ayahs.translation: And We did certainly send Moses with Our signs and a clear authority
ayahs.summary: We did certainly send Musa with Our signs and a clear authority.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Nation after nation. We did certainly send Musa with Our signs and a clear authority.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Every nation received signs and clear authority from Allah; the people of Musa were no exception.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Madyan were taken away as Thamud.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: To Pharaoh and his establishment; they followed the command of Pharaoh.

ayahs.arabic: إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَإِي۟هِۦ فَٱتَّبَعُوٓا۟ أَمْرَ فِرْعَوْنَ ۖ وَمَآ أَمْرُ فِرْعَوْنَ بِرَشِيدٍۢ ٩٧
ayahs.translation: To Pharaoh and his establishment, but they followed the command of Pharaoh, and the command of Pharaoh was not [at all] discerning.
ayahs.summary: To Pharaoh and his establishment—they followed the command of Pharaoh, and the command of Pharaoh was not discerning.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: To Pharaoh and his establishment, but they followed the command of Pharaoh, and the command of Pharaoh was not at all discerning.
ayahs.lessonReflection: We must see who we follow today—whoever we follow now is the one we will be with in the Hereafter.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Musa was sent with signs and clear authority.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: He will precede his people on the Day of Resurrection and lead them into the Fire.

ayahs.arabic: يَقْدُمُ قَوْمَهُۥ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ فَأَوْرَدَهُمُ ٱلنَّارَ ۖ وَبِئْسَ ٱلْوِرْدُ ٱلْمَوْرُودُ ٩٨
ayahs.translation: He will precede his people on the Day of Resurrection and lead them into the Fire; and wretched is the place to which they are led.
ayahs.summary: He will precede his people on the Day of Resurrection and lead them into the Fire; wretched is the place to which they are led.

wordHighlights.translation: Place to which they are led

ayahs.tafsirSummary: Those who followed Pharaoh and did not follow Musa—Pharaoh will lead them into the Fire on the Day of Resurrection. Wretched is the place to which they are led.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Who we follow in this world will lead us in the next—Pharaoh leads his followers into the Fire.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: They followed Pharaoh's command.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: They were followed in this world with a curse and on the Day of Resurrection.

ayahs.arabic: وَأُتْبِعُوا۟ فِى هَـٰذِهِۦ لَعْنَةًۭ وَيَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۚ بِئْسَ ٱلرِّفْدُ ٱلْمَرْفُودُ ٩٩
ayahs.translation: And they were followed in this [world] with a curse and on the Day of Resurrection. And wretched is the gift which is given.
ayahs.summary: They were followed in this world with a curse and on the Day of Resurrection. Wretched is the gift which is given.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Whenever a person follows somebody he expects some benefit. They followed Pharaoh—what do they get? A curse in this world and on the Day of Resurrection. Wretched is the gift which is given.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Following the wrong leader brings a curse and a wretched gift—not the benefit they expected.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Pharaoh will lead them into the Fire.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: That is from the news of the cities which We relate; some are standing and some are a harvest.

ayahs.arabic: ذَٰلِكَ مِنْ أَنۢبَآءِ ٱلْقُرَىٰ نَقُصُّهُۥ عَلَيْكَ ۖ مِنْهَا قَآئِمٌۭ وَحَصِيدٌۭ ١٠٠
ayahs.translation: That is from the news of the cities, which We relate to you; of them, some are [still] standing and some are [as] a harvest [mowed down].
ayahs.summary: That is from the news of the cities which We relate to you; of them some are standing and some are as a harvest mowed down.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Some of these past peoples—you see their homes standing today, like the pyramids. Others you do not see their traces anymore; you do not even know where they lived.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The cities of the past are a lesson—some ruins remain, some are gone; all are news for us.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: They received curse and wretched gift.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: We did not wrong them but they wronged themselves; their gods did not avail them.

ayahs.arabic: وَمَا ظَلَمْنَـٰهُمْ وَلَـٰكِن ظَلَمُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ ۖ فَمَآ أَغْنَتْ عَنْهُمْ ءَالِهَتُهُمُ ٱلَّتِى يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مِن شَىْءٍۢ لَّمَّا جَآءَ أَمْرُ رَبِّكَ ۖ وَمَا زَادُوهُمْ غَيْرَ تَتْبِيبٍۢ ١٠١
ayahs.translation: And We did not wrong them, but they wronged themselves. And they were not availed at all by their gods which they invoked other than Allāh when there came the command of your Lord. And they did not increase them in other than ruin.
ayahs.summary: We did not wrong them but they wronged themselves. Their gods did not avail them when the command of your Lord came; they did not increase them except in ruin.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: We did not wrong them but they wronged themselves. Their gods which they invoked other than Allah did not avail them at all when the command of your Lord came; they did not increase them except in ruin.
ayahs.lessonReflection: When the command comes, only Allah avails; false gods add nothing but ruin.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The news of the cities was related.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Thus is the seizure of your Lord when He seizes the cities while they are wronging.

ayahs.arabic: وَكَذَٰلِكَ أَخْذُ رَبِّكَ إِذَآ أَخَذَ ٱلْقُرَىٰ وَهِىَ ظَـٰلِمَةٌ ۚ إِنَّ أَخْذَهُۥٓ أَلِيمٌۭ شَدِيدٌ ١٠٢
ayahs.translation: And thus is the seizure of your Lord when He seizes the cities while they are committing wrong. Indeed, His seizure is painful and severe.
ayahs.summary: Thus is the seizure of your Lord when He seizes the cities while they are wronging. His seizure is painful and severe.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Allah gives respite to the oppressor—He gives time and chances. One nation was given nine hundred fifty years. But when He takes them, when He seizes them, He never releases them; that is the end. We must reflect: how many chances has our Lord given us? We must avail those chances and not be deceived by this world.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Allah gives respite, but when He seizes the wrongdoing city His seizure is painful and severe with no release.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: They were not availed by their gods.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: In that is a sign for those who fear the punishment of the Hereafter.

ayahs.arabic: إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةًۭ لِّمَنْ خَافَ عَذَابَ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ يَوْمٌۭ مَّجْمُوعٌۭ لَّهُ ٱلنَّاسُ وَذَٰلِكَ يَوْمٌۭ مَّشْهُودٌۭ ١٠٣
ayahs.translation: Indeed in that is a sign for those who fear the punishment of the Hereafter. That is a Day for which the people will be collected, and that is a Day [which will be] witnessed.
ayahs.summary: In that is a sign for those who fear the punishment of the Hereafter. That is a Day for which people will be collected and a Day witnessed.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Indeed in that is a sign for those who fear the punishment of the Hereafter. That is a Day for which the people will be collected and that is a Day witnessed—by all people and by Us.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The fate of the cities is a sign for whoever fears the punishment of the Hereafter; that Day is witnessed by all.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: His seizure was described as painful and severe.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: We do not delay it except for a limited term.

ayahs.arabic: وَمَا نُؤَخِّرُهُۥٓ إِلَّا لِأَجَلٍۢ مَّعْدُودٍۢ ١٠٤
ayahs.translation: And We do not delay it except for a limited term.
ayahs.summary: We do not delay it except for a limited term.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: We do not delay it except for a limited term. It is not far.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The Day is not delayed except for a numbered term; it is near.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: That Day was described as collected and witnessed.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: The Day it comes no soul will speak except by His permission.

ayahs.arabic: يَوْمَ يَأْتِ لَا تَكَلَّمُ نَفْسٌ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِۦ ۚ فَمِنْهُمْ شَقِىٌّۭ وَسَعِيدٌۭ ١٠٥
ayahs.translation: The Day it comes no soul will speak except by His permission. And among them will be the wretched and the prosperous.
ayahs.summary: The Day it comes no soul will speak except by His permission. Among them will be the wretched and the prosperous.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: The Day it comes no soul will speak except by His permission. And among them will be the wretched and the prosperous.
ayahs.lessonReflection: On that Day no one speaks without permission; the division is between the wretched and the prosperous.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The delay is only for a limited term.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: As for the wretched they will be in the Fire with violent exhaling and inhaling.

ayahs.arabic: فَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ شَقُوا۟ فَفِى ٱلنَّارِ لَهُمْ فِيهَا زَفِيرٌۭ وَشَهِيقٌ ١٠٦
ayahs.translation: As for those who were [destined to be] wretched, they will be in the Fire. For them therein is [violent] exhaling and inhaling.
ayahs.summary: As for those who were wretched they will be in the Fire; for them therein is violent exhaling and inhaling.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: As for those who were wretched they will be in the Fire; for them therein is violent exhaling and inhaling.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The wretched have only the Fire and its sounds of distress.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: No soul will speak except by His permission.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: They will abide therein as long as the heavens and the earth endure except what your Lord wills.

ayahs.arabic: خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا مَا دَامَتِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضُ إِلَّا مَا شَآءَ رَبُّكَ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ فَعَّالٌۭ لِّمَا يُرِيدُ ١٠٧
ayahs.translation: [They will be] abiding therein as long as the heavens and the earth endure, except what your Lord should will. Indeed, your Lord is an effecter of what He intends.
ayahs.summary: They will abide therein as long as the heavens and the earth endure except what your Lord wills. Your Lord is an effecter of what He intends.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: If He wills He can take some out of the Fire; He will eventually take some people out, not all. Indeed your Lord is an effecter of what He intends.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Even in the Fire, Allah's will prevails; He can take out whom He wills. He is the effecter of what He intends.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The wretched were described as in the Fire.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: As for the prosperous they will be in Paradise, abiding therein.

ayahs.arabic: ۞ وَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ سُعِدُوا۟ فَفِى ٱلْجَنَّةِ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا مَا دَامَتِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضُ إِلَّا مَا شَآءَ رَبُّكَ ۖ عَطَآءً غَيْرَ مَجْذُوذٍۢ ١٠٨
ayahs.translation: And as for those who were [destined to be] prosperous, they will be in Paradise, abiding therein as long as the heavens and the earth endure, except what your Lord should will - a bestowal uninterrupted.
ayahs.summary: As for those who were prosperous they will be in Paradise, abiding therein as long as the heavens and the earth endure except what your Lord wills—a bestowal uninterrupted.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Even when they abide eternally in Jannah He is still their Lord; nothing happens except with His will. A bestowal uninterrupted.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The prosperous have Paradise as an uninterrupted bestowal; He remains their Lord forever.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The wretched abide in the Fire except what Allah wills.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Do not be in doubt as to what these polytheists worship; they worship only as their fathers did.

ayahs.arabic: فَلَا تَكُ فِى مِرْيَةٍۢ مِّمَّا يَعْبُدُ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ ۚ مَا يَعْبُدُونَ إِلَّا كَمَا يَعْبُدُ ءَابَآؤُهُم مِّن قَبْلُ ۚ وَإِنَّا لَمُوَفُّوهُمْ نَصِيبَهُمْ غَيْرَ مَنقُوصٍۢ ١٠٩
ayahs.translation: So do not be in doubt, [O Muḥammad], as to what these [polytheists] are worshipping. They worship not except as their fathers worshipped before. And indeed, We will give them their share undiminished.
ayahs.summary: Do not be in doubt as to what these worship. They worship only as their fathers worshipped before. We will give them their share undiminished.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: They are only blindly following. And indeed We will give them their share undiminished.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The polytheists only imitate their fathers; they will receive their full share (of punishment).
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The prosperous were described in Paradise.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: We gave Musa the Scripture but it came under disagreement.

ayahs.arabic: وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا مُوسَى ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ فَٱخْتُلِفَ فِيهِ ۚ وَلَوْلَا كَلِمَةٌۭ سَبَقَتْ مِن رَّبِّكَ لَقُضِىَ بَيْنَهُمْ ۚ وَإِنَّهُمْ لَفِى شَكٍّۢ مِّنْهُ مُرِيبٍۢ ١١٠
ayahs.translation: And We had certainly given Moses the Scripture, but it came under disagreement. And if not for a word that preceded from your Lord, it would have been judged between them. And indeed they are, concerning it [i.e., the Qur'ān], in disquieting doubt.
ayahs.summary: We gave Musa the Scripture but it came under disagreement. If not for a word that preceded from your Lord it would have been judged between them. They are concerning the Qur'an in disquieting doubt.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: We had given Musa the Scripture but it came under disagreement. If not for a word that preceded from your Lord it would have been judged between them. They are concerning the Qur'an in disquieting doubt.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Previous scriptures were disputed; the Qur'an is clear. Their doubt is disquieting and will be judged.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The Prophet was told not to be in doubt about what they worship.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Each—your Lord will fully compensate them for their deeds.

ayahs.arabic: وَإِنَّ كُلًّۭا لَّمَّا لَيُوَفِّيَنَّهُمْ رَبُّكَ أَعْمَـٰلَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌۭ ١١١
ayahs.translation: And indeed, each [of the believers and disbelievers] - your Lord will fully compensate them for their deeds. Indeed, He is Aware of what they do.
ayahs.summary: Each—your Lord will fully compensate them for their deeds. Indeed He is Aware of what they do.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Including you and I. Nothing is hidden of our actions. Your Lord will fully compensate them for their deeds; He is Acquainted with what they do.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Every soul will be paid in full for its deeds; He is aware of all we do.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The Scripture of Musa and their doubt were mentioned.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: So remain on a right course as you have been commanded.

ayahs.arabic: فَٱسْتَقِمْ كَمَآ أُمِرْتَ وَمَن تَابَ مَعَكَ وَلَا تَطْغَوْا۟ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌۭ ١١٢
ayahs.translation: So remain on a right course as you have been commanded, [you] and those who have turned back with you [to Allāh], and do not transgress. Indeed, He is Seeing of what you do.
ayahs.summary: So remain on a right course as you have been commanded, you and those who have turned back with you, and do not transgress. He is Seeing of what you do.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Become firm as Allah has ordered you; whatever He has ordered, do it properly and do not stop. And those who have turned back with you to Allah—and do not transgress. Indeed He is Seeing of what you do.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Steadfastness on the commanded path and not transgressing—He sees all we do.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Each will be fully compensated for their deeds.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Do not incline toward those who do wrong lest the Fire touch you.

ayahs.arabic: وَلَا تَرْكَنُوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ فَتَمَسَّكُمُ ٱلنَّارُ وَمَا لَكُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مِنْ أَوْلِيَآءَ ثُمَّ لَا تُنصَرُونَ ١١٣
ayahs.translation: And do not incline toward those who do wrong, lest you be touched by the Fire, and you would not have other than Allāh any protectors; then you would not be helped.
ayahs.summary: Do not incline toward those who do wrong lest the Fire touch you; you would have no protectors besides Allah and would not be helped.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Do not incline toward those who do wrong lest you be touched by the Fire. You would not have other than Allah any protectors; then you would not be helped.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Inclining to the wrongdoers risks the Fire; without Allah we have no protector and no help.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The Prophet was told to remain steadfast.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Establish prayer at the two ends of the day and at the approach of the night.

ayahs.arabic: وَأَقِمِ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ طَرَفَىِ ٱلنَّهَارِ وَزُلَفًۭا مِّنَ ٱلَّيْلِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلْحَسَنَـٰتِ يُذْهِبْنَ ٱلسَّيِّـَٔاتِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ ذِكْرَىٰ لِلذَّٰكِرِينَ ١١٤
ayahs.translation: And establish prayer at the two ends of the day and at the approach of the night. Indeed, good deeds do away with misdeeds. That is a reminder for those who remember.
ayahs.summary: Establish prayer at the two ends of the day and at the approach of the night. Good deeds do away with misdeeds. That is a reminder for those who remember.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Two ends of the day—Fajr and Maghrib. And at the approach of the night—Isha. Indeed good deeds do away with misdeeds. Not just sadaqah and salah—all good deeds erase sins. The Prophet said: The example of one who commits sins then performs good deeds is like a man with a tight armor; each good deed breaks a link until he is free. When you do a bad deed follow it with a good deed; saying la ilaha illallah is the best of good deeds.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Prayer at Fajr, Maghrib, and Isha; good deeds erase misdeeds. That is a reminder for those who remember.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Do not incline toward the wrongdoers.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Be patient; Allah does not allow the reward of the doers of good to be lost.

ayahs.arabic: وَٱصْبِرْ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُضِيعُ أَجْرَ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ ١١٥
ayahs.translation: And be patient, for indeed, Allāh does not allow to be lost the reward of those who do good.
ayahs.summary: And be patient; indeed Allah does not allow to be lost the reward of those who do good.

wordHighlights.translation: Reward of those who do good

ayahs.tafsirSummary: Life is difficult but be patient. To do good you need patience—control yourself and fight the environment. When you keep doing good that is patience. Allah does not waste the rewards of those who do good.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Patience in doing good is never wasted; Allah does not allow the reward of the muhsinin to be lost.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Prayer and good deeds erasing misdeeds were mentioned.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Why were there not among the generations before you those forbidding corruption?

ayahs.arabic: فَلَوْلَا كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْقُرُونِ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ أُو۟لُوا۟ بَقِيَّةٍۢ يَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ ٱلْفَسَادِ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ إِلَّا قَلِيلًۭا مِّمَّنْ أَنجَيْنَا مِنْهُمْ ۗ وَٱتَّبَعَ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ مَآ أُتْرِفُوا۟ فِيهِ وَكَانُوا۟ مُجْرِمِينَ ١١٦
ayahs.translation: So why were there not among the generations before you those of enduring discrimination forbidding corruption on earth - except a few of those We saved from among them? But those who wronged pursued what luxury they were given therein, and they were criminals.
ayahs.summary: Why were there not among the generations before you those forbidding corruption on earth except a few We saved? Those who wronged pursued the luxury they were given and were criminals.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Allah demands that there must be some people who forbid corruption in the land. Where are they? Just as a community needs police and firefighters, we need those who stop people from shirk and disobedience. Why do we keep throwing this on others? Why not me? What prevents us? Our dunya, our houses. Those who wronged pursued the luxury they were given and were criminals.
ayahs.lessonReflection: We are responsible to forbid corruption; we cannot leave it only to others. Our comfort often holds us back.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Patience and the reward of the doers of good were stated.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Your Lord would not have destroyed the cities unjustly while their people were reformers.

ayahs.arabic: وَمَا كَانَ رَبُّكَ لِيُهْلِكَ ٱلْقُرَىٰ بِظُلْمٍۢ وَأَهْلُهَا مُصْلِحُونَ ١١٧
ayahs.translation: And your Lord would not have destroyed the cities unjustly while their people were reformers.
ayahs.summary: Your Lord would not have destroyed the cities unjustly while their people were reformers.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Your Lord would not have destroyed the cities unjustly while their people were reformers.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Allah does not destroy a people who are reformers; destruction comes when they abandon reform.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The lack of those forbidding corruption was mentioned.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: If your Lord had willed He could have made mankind one community.

ayahs.arabic: وَلَوْ شَآءَ رَبُّكَ لَجَعَلَ ٱلنَّاسَ أُمَّةًۭ وَٰحِدَةًۭ ۖ وَلَا يَزَالُونَ مُخْتَلِفِينَ ١١٨
ayahs.translation: And if your Lord had willed, He could have made mankind one community; but they will not cease to differ,
ayahs.summary: If your Lord had willed He could have made mankind one community; but they will not cease to differ.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: If your Lord had willed He could have made mankind one community; but they will not cease to differ.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Allah could have made us one; He allowed difference. Our duty is to hold to the truth and invite to it.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: Allah would not destroy cities whose people were reformers.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Except whom your Lord has given mercy; and for that He created them.

ayahs.arabic: إِلَّا مَن رَّحِمَ رَبُّكَ ۚ وَلِذَٰلِكَ خَلَقَهُمْ ۗ وَتَمَّتْ كَلِمَةُ رَبِّكَ لَأَمْلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ مِنَ ٱلْجِنَّةِ وَٱلنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ ١١٩
ayahs.translation: Except whom your Lord has given mercy, and for that He created them. But the word of your Lord is to be fulfilled that, "I will surely fill Hell with jinn and men all together."
ayahs.summary: Except whom your Lord has given mercy; and for that He created them. The word of your Lord is fulfilled: I will surely fill Hell with jinn and men all together.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: Except whom your Lord has given mercy—and for that He created them. But the word of your Lord is to be fulfilled: I will surely fill Hell with jinn and men all together.
ayahs.lessonReflection: Mercy is for those He chooses; the rest fill Hell as His word is fulfilled. We seek to be among the merciful.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: They will not cease to differ.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: Each story We relate from the news of the messengers is that by which We make firm your heart.

ayahs.arabic: وَكُلًّۭا نَّقُصُّ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ أَنۢبَآءِ ٱلرُّسُلِ مَا نُثَبِّتُ بِهِۦ فُؤَادَكَ ۚ وَجَآءَكَ فِى هَـٰذِهِ ٱلْحَقُّ وَمَوْعِظَةٌۭ وَذِكْرَىٰ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ١٢٠
ayahs.translation: And each [story] We relate to you from the news of the messengers is that by which We make firm your heart. And there has come to you, in this, the truth and an instruction and a reminder for the believers.
ayahs.summary: Each story We relate from the news of the messengers is that by which We make firm your heart. In this has come to you the truth and an instruction and a reminder for the believers.
ayahs.tafsirSummary: When you see the struggles of the prophets it strengthens your heart and gives you stability and firmness. And there has come to you in this the truth and an instruction and a reminder for the believers.
ayahs.lessonReflection: The stories of the messengers steady our hearts; in this surah we have the truth, instruction, and a reminder for the believers.
ayahs.beforeThisAyah: The word of your Lord about filling Hell was stated.
ayahs.afterThisAyah: This closes the surah.
